papers.title: An electronic fingerprint device based on spiral patterned tactile pixel array for augmented human-machine interactions
papers.abstract_snippet: Nature Communications, Published online: 31 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-73973-6 The advancement of human–machine interfaces relies on wearable tactile devices that combine flexibility with portability. Jin et al. present an electronic fingerprint device capable of simultaneously detecting normal force, slippage direction, and displacement, enabling enhanced human–robot interactions.
papers.reason: 该论文主要介绍一种基于螺旋图案触觉像素阵列的电子指纹设备，侧重于可穿戴人机接口的硬件传感技术，而非机器人操作或 VLA 算法。缺乏与具身智能控制、策略学习或视觉语言动作模型相关的实验或方法论述。
